name: Acetophenazine
description: Acetophenazine is an antipsychotic drug of moderate-potency. It is used in the treatment of disorganized and psychotic thinking. It is also used to help treat false perceptions (e.g. hallucinations or delusions). It primarily targets the dopamine D2 receptor.
indication: For the treatment of disorganized and psychotic thinking. Also used to help treat false perceptions (e.g. hallucinations or delusions.)
mechanismOfAction: Acetophenazine blocks postsynaptic mesolimbic dopaminergic D1 and D2 receptors in the brain; depresses the release of hypothalamic and hypophyseal hormones and is believed to depress the reticular activating system thus affecting basal metabolism, body temperature, wakefulness, vasomotor tone, and emesis.
pharmacodynamics: Acetophenzine is a phenothiazine antipsychotic intended for the management of schizophrenia and other psychotic disorders.

interactions.name: Vemurafenib
interactions.description: The risk or severity of QTc prolongation can be increased when Vemurafenib is combined with Acetophenazine.

interactions.name: Amisulpride
interactions.description: Acetophenazine may increase the antipsychotic activities of Amisulpride.

interactions.name: Metoclopramide
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Metoclopramide is combined with Acetophenazine.

interactions.name: Sulpiride
interactions.description: Acetophenazine may increase the antipsychotic activities of Sulpiride.

interactions.name: Mequitazine
interactions.description: Acetophenazine may increase the arrhythmogenic activities of Mequitazine.

interactions.name: Quinagolide
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Quinagolide can be decreased when used in combination with Acetophenazine.

interactions.name: Lithium
interactions.description: Lithium may increase the neurotoxic activities of Acetophenazine.
